title: STAT+: Trump can’t break free from vaccine politics
author: John Wilkerson
fullText:
You’re reading STAT’s D.C. Diagnosis newsletter
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 SAUL LOEB / AFP via Getty Images

 
 John Wilkerson is a Washington correspondent for STAT who writes about the politics of health care. He is also the author of the twice-weekly D.C. Diagnosis newsletter.

 You’re reading the web edition of D.C. Diagnosis, STAT’s twice-weekly newsletter about the politics and policy of health and medicine. Sign up here to receive it in your inbox on Tuesdays and Thursdays.

And you may find yourself climbing a StairMaster next to RFK Jr. Send news tips and vague Talking Heads references to [email protected] or John_Wilkerson.07 on Signal. Also, don’t forget to fill out your STAT Madness brackets.

The pull of vaccine politics

The Trump administration is trying to shift focus away from controversial vaccine policies toward topics that are popular with voters, such as lowering drug prices and encouraging healthy diets. But vaccines keep coming up in the health care news cycle.

 John Wilkerson is a Washington correspondent for STAT who writes about the politics of health care. He is also the author of the twice-weekly D.C. Diagnosis newsletter.
summary: Trump is trying to shift the focus toward healthy eating and cheaper drugs. But vaccines keeping coming back up.
